steps.text: Install the CEMS at an accessible location downstream of all pollution control equipment.
Select representative measurement points or paths for monitoring in locations that the CEMS will pass the RA test.
Determine measurement locations that provide data that will meet the RA requirements.
Ensure the measurement location is at least eight equivalent diameters downstream of the nearest control device.
Ensure the measurement location is at least two equivalent diameters upstream from the effluent exhaust.
Ensure the measurement point is no less than 1.0 meter from the stack or duct wall.
Ensure measurement point is centrally located over the centroidal area of the stack or duct cross section.
Select traverse points to determine the representativeness of the measurement location.
Ensure the effective measurement path is totally within the inner area bounded by a line 1.0 meter from the stack or duct wall.
Ensure at least 70 percent of the measurement path is within the inner 50 percent of the stack or duct cross sectional area.
Ensure the measurement path is centrally located over any part of the centroidal area.
Install the CEMS and prepare the RM test site according to the specifications in Section 3.
Prepare the CEMS for operation according to the manufacturer’s written instructions.
Determine the magnitude of the calibration drift (CD) and zero drift (ZD) once each day (at 24-hour intervals) for 7 consecutive days.
Conduct a RA test following the CD test period.
Conduct the RA test according to the procedure given in Section 7 while the affected facility is operating at more than 50 percent of normal load, or as specified in the applicable subpart.
Conduct the CD test immediately before the adjustments.
Conduct the CD and ZD tests at the points specified in Sections 4.3 and 4.4.
Record the CEMS response and calculate the CD.
Calculate the ZD according to the provided formulas.
Conduct the diluent, moisture, and pollutant measurements simultaneously if applicable.
Take diluent and moisture measurements within a 30- to 60-minute period.
Calculate dry pollutant concentration using the measurements taken.
Determine the concentration of each metal to be detectable by both the CEMS and the RM.
Determine RA at three levels for one of the metals or for iron.
Correlate the CEMS and RM data by noting the beginning and end of each RM test period.
Determine the integrated average pollutant concentration from the CEMS final output.
Confirm that the pair of results are on a consistent moisture, temperature, and diluent concentration basis.
Compare each integrated CEMS value against the corresponding average RM value.
Obtain a minimum of three pairs of CEMS and RM measurements for each metal required and at each level required.
Report all data, including the rejected data.
Summarize the results on a data sheet.
Calculate the mean of the RM values.
Calculate the arithmetic differences between the RM and CEMS output sets, and then calculate the mean of the differences.
Calculate the standard deviation of each data set and CEMS RA using the equations in Section 8.

preconditions.text: If the RM traverse line interferes with or is interfered by the CEMS measurements, the line may be displaced up to 30 cm (or 5 percent of the equivalent diameter of the cross section, whichever is less) from the centroidal area.
While the affected facility is operating at more than 50 percent of normal load, or as specified in an applicable subpart.
Determinations must be made following a 24-hour period during which no unscheduled maintenance, repair, or manual adjustment of the CEMS took place.
If iron is chosen, the three levels should correspond to those for one of the metals monitored.

constraints.text: None of the CD’s or ZD’s may exceed the specification.
